jokes.text: Unfortunately, due to budget constraints, this year we will have to remove five jewels from that crown.
jokes.setup: Leslie calls recreation classes 'jewels in Pawnee's beautiful crown'
jokes.punchline: Immediately has to announce cutting them due to budget cuts
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic juxtaposition with Leslie's optimistic metaphor immediately undercut by harsh reality. Economical setup/payoff.

jokes.text: My class is called 'Coping with Terminal Illness.'
jokes.setup: Leslie says make sure students leave with a smile
jokes.punchline: Teacher reveals their class is about terminal illness
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - the absurdity of making terminal illness students leave with smiles is perfectly economical.

jokes.text: Hopefully your attendance is good! Actually, no, hopefully it's bad.
jokes.setup: Leslie encourages good attendance
jokes.punchline: Realizes terminal illness class should have bad attendance
jokes.explanation: Leslie's well-meaning nature creating awkward logical contradictions - good character comedy.

jokes.text: It's where I learned hair braiding, and how to make biscuits, and French kissing. The French kissing was just from a boy in my biscuits class, but either way, lesson learned.
jokes.setup: Leslie lists skills learned at rec center including French kissing
jokes.punchline: Reveals it wasn't an official class, just a boy in biscuits class
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie overshare with innocent misdirection. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: That fish over there kind of reminds me of my mom. Why? It's just being really withholding.
jokes.setup: Leslie comments that fish reminds her of her mom
jokes.punchline: Because the fish is being withholding
jokes.explanation: Subtle character moment revealing Leslie's family dynamics through aquarium observation.

jokes.text: Isn't it great? It's camel stomach. Is it? I'm teasing, I'm teasing. It's chicken.
jokes.setup: Justin offers food, claims it's camel stomach
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's joking, it's just chicken
jokes.explanation: Standard fake-out joke, nothing particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: I've racked my brain and I can't come up with one exciting idea.
jokes.setup: Leslie confidently promises Pawnee date will blow Indianapolis out of water
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals in talking head she has no ideas
jokes.explanation: Good use of mockumentary format to reveal gap between public confidence and private panic.

jokes.text: Well, maybe just have him over and cook him some chicken and tell him it's Pawnee cockatoo.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions Justin ate fried cockatoo with Thai royalty
jokes.punchline: Ann suggests fake exotic dish with local bird name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on local vs. exotic, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: No, I actually meant more like a one-on-one thing. I know what you meant, but I took your idea and I made it better. It's called a think tank, Ann.
jokes.setup: Ann suggests intimate dinner, Leslie suggests dinner party
jokes.punchline: Leslie claims she improved Ann's idea and calls their lunches think tanks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - overcomplicating simple things and rebranding normal activities.

jokes.text: How many courses will there be? Three. Four. Not including dessert. So, five courses. Yes. Now, it will be five courses.
jokes.setup: Ron asks about number of courses
jokes.punchline: Leslie keeps increasing the count in real time
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation showing Leslie's tendency to overcomplicate under pressure.

jokes.text: If I wanted to bring a large number of deviled eggs, but I didn't want to share them with anyone else, can you guarantee fridge space?
jokes.setup: Ron asking practical party question
jokes.punchline: Wants to bring food specifically not to share
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Ron's antisocial nature - attending a party but refusing to participate socially.

jokes.text: And you, out of all my friends, come from the most distant and exotic land. South Carolina?
jokes.setup: Leslie builds up Tom's exotic origins
jokes.punchline: South Carolina is hardly exotic
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection on Leslie's Pawnee-centric worldview where South Carolina seems exotic.

jokes.text: What are you guys talking about? Nothing. Don't worry about it. What?
jokes.setup: Tom tells Leslie not to invite Jerry, Jerry appears
jokes.punchline: Awkward dismissal of Jerry's innocent question
jokes.explanation: Standard Jerry exclusion moment - mildly uncomfortable but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: Hey, it's me, Justin. Take my coat. But please, be careful. I got it from the king of Africa when we were walking on the Berlin Wall together.
jokes.setup: April practicing taking coats
jokes.punchline: Absurd fake Justin backstory combining impossible elements
jokes.explanation: Great April moment - deadpan delivery of deliberately impossible combination of exotic elements.

jokes.text: Hi, Kyle. You know, right before you sat down, I noticed, did you have some kind of stain on your ass, or something? What was that?
jokes.setup: Andy nervously greeting someone after polishing benches
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asks about stain on their butt
jokes.explanation: Andy's awkward social skills on display - references earlier bench polishing concern.

jokes.text: Now, I can believe it.
jokes.setup: Ann sees Leslie's house for first time, Leslie says she can't believe Ann hasn't been over
jokes.punchline: Ann's deadpan reaction to the messy house
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - Ann's understated reaction to obvious visual comedy setup.

jokes.text: This newspaper's from November, 1986. Oh, the first rumblings of Iran-Contra! Don't throw that out!
jokes.setup: Ann finds old newspaper while cleaning
jokes.punchline: Leslie wants to keep it for historical significance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - finding historical importance in hoarding behavior.

jokes.text: I think I need to call Child Services and have Leslie taken away from herself.
jokes.setup: Ann surveying Leslie's messy house
jokes.punchline: Treating adult Leslie like a child who needs intervention
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation of the 'messy house' premise with absurd solution.

jokes.text: Your house is like a crazy person's garage.
jokes.setup: Ann trying to be diplomatic about Leslie's house
jokes.punchline: Specific, vivid comparison that's not actually diplomatic
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific insult that captures the visual while maintaining Ann's attempted diplomacy.

jokes.text: I might need it. What about this one? Well, if two birds come along?
jokes.setup: Ann questions keeping one birdhouse
jokes.punchline: Leslie's logic for keeping a second birdhouse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of hoarder logic - justifying excess with hypothetical scenarios.

jokes.text: Shake my hand. Why? I can't tell if I've exfoliated too much. I don't want to creep Justin out. I want him to respect my handshake.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking April to shake her hand
jokes.punchline: Overthinking handshake texture for Justin
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's neurotic attention to bizarre details when nervous.

jokes.text: People in this town are just now getting into Nirvana. I don't have the heart to tell them what's going to happen to Kurt Cobain in 1994.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining how unhip Pawnee is
jokes.punchline: Reference to Kurt Cobain's death as if it's future knowledge
jokes.explanation: Clever dark comedy mixing Leslie's earnestness with tragic real-world knowledge. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Ew! It's like touching raw chicken. Damn it. Gonna have to re-foliate them.
jokes.setup: April testing Leslie's handshake texture
jokes.punchline: Disgusted reaction and Leslie's made-up word 'refoliate'
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to handshake setup, plus Leslie's malapropism 'refoliate.'

jokes.text: Oh! No, no, I insist on paying. No, no, I insist on demonstrating. This is not gonna affect my decision at all. Oh, of course not. Stop winking.
jokes.setup: Teacher offering free help to demonstrate class value
jokes.punchline: Awkward back-and-forth ending with Ron telling them to stop winking
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with escalating awkwardness and Ron's blunt intervention.

jokes.text: Five courses. Of what? Huh? Of what? Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Leslie confidently announces five courses
jokes.punchline: Teacher's simple question reveals Leslie hasn't planned the menu
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier course counting - Leslie's planning is all ambition, no details.

jokes.text: You've been here? Right.
jokes.setup: Mark mentions Leslie's house is cleaner than last time
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward realization Mark has been there before
jokes.explanation: Subtle awkwardness implying romantic history without explicitly stating it.

jokes.text: Oh! Sex.
jokes.setup: Ann processing when Mark was at Leslie's before
jokes.punchline: Blunt realization delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - maximum comedic impact with minimal dialogue. Ann's medical directness applied to social situation.

jokes.text: That was after I told him that I sometimes go swimming at the Y.
jokes.setup: Mark describing Justin's exotic whale shark story
jokes.punchline: Mark's mundane contribution to the conversation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between exotic adventure and mundane Pawnee reality.

jokes.text: No, no, no! No talking. Everybody stop talking until Justin gets here. Don't use up your stories.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to manage party conversation
jokes.punchline: Treating stories like a finite resource that can be 'used up'
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie logic - treating conversation like a strategic resource to be managed.

jokes.text: Please, have a good time and shut your mouth.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving party instructions
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instruction to have fun while being quiet
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction showing Leslie's stress manifesting as impossible demands.

jokes.text: There are very few things I've asked for in this world. To build a new park from scratch. To eventually become president. And to one day solve a murder on a train.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing her life goals
jokes.punchline: Escalation from reasonable to presidential to absurd mystery novel fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - mixing professional ambition with childlike fantasy. Great escalation structure.

jokes.text: Really, really, just like the best, most exciting night of your life. I cannot over-hype it enough.
jokes.setup: Leslie promising Justin an amazing night
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging she's over-hyping while continuing to over-hype
jokes.explanation: Leslie's self-awareness doesn't stop her behavior - good character consistency.

jokes.text: I have the swine flu. Oh... No, she doesn't.
jokes.setup: Ann introducing herself to Justin
jokes.punchline: Announces illness, Leslie immediately corrects
jokes.explanation: Awkward social moment - Ann's medical background creating inappropriate party conversation.

jokes.text: He got it in Cambodia, when he was hiking Mount Everest.
jokes.setup: Andy admiring Justin's coat
jokes.punchline: Geographically impossible story mixing Cambodia and Everest
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - mixing up exotic locations in attempt to sound impressive.

jokes.text: I'm being sarcastic. That would be awesome.
jokes.setup: Andy saying putting gum in coat would be terrible
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he was being sarcastic and would enjoy sabotage
jokes.explanation: Andy's childlike honesty about wanting to cause mischief.

jokes.text: Are you kidding? My uncle practically runs the place. I've prayed there. It's sick.
jokes.setup: Justin asking about specific Indian mosque
jokes.punchline: Tom making up connection he doesn't have
jokes.explanation: Tom's desperation to seem cultured leading to obvious lies.

jokes.text: The last time I was in India, I was eight years old and I stayed inside the whole time playing video games. I got to bone up.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to impress Justin with Indian knowledge
jokes.punchline: Admitting he knows nothing and frantically researching
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Tom's pretense - honest about his actual non-experience.

jokes.text: So my face is literally on fire, but it's this weird chemical, so no one can see the flames. I'm dancing around, freaking out. I end up having to dunk my face into a bucket of sand.
jokes.setup: Mark telling story to impress Justin
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd physical comedy scenario
jokes.explanation: Decent visual comedy story, though not particularly character-specific for Mark.

jokes.text: Didn't happen to me. It was a kid I knew from camp. But a great story's a great story. Well, plus, the kid can't tell it, his mouth melted.
jokes.setup: Mark admits story wasn't his experience
jokes.punchline: Darkly jokes about victim's inability to tell their own story
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark escalation - casual cruelty disguised as storytelling pragmatism.

jokes.text: How could you invite Wendy when Ron's here? He's gonna wrap her up in his moustache and take her home.
jokes.setup: Tom upset about Wendy being invited
jokes.punchline: Absurd visual metaphor about Ron's mustache as predatory tool
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor combining Ron's iconic mustache with Tom's jealousy fears. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Don't do that, Tom!
jokes.setup: Tom eating extremely hot pepper and performing
jokes.punchline: Ron's urgent warning (implied after Tom's already in distress)
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy reaction - Ron's too-late warning.

jokes.text: Derek and Ben are gay, but often, on occasion, April will have relations with Derek. Right? Crazy stuff. Discuss that.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to create interesting conversation
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly oversharing others' private business as conversation starter
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Leslie's desperation making her violate social norms.

jokes.text: Who else is gay?
jokes.setup: Awkward conversation about Derek and Ben
jokes.punchline: Leslie trying to continue inappropriate topic
jokes.explanation: Escalation of previous cringe moment - Leslie doubling down on inappropriate conversation.

jokes.text: Poor Tommy. He has a very frail colon.
jokes.setup: Tom taking long time in bathroom after hot peppers
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact medical assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron delivery - deadpan concern with specific medical terminology.

jokes.text: A belly dancer? What? It's weird, but she just showed up. I mean, I'm not the kind of person that's gonna throw someone out, or, you know, not let them in if they randomly show up.
jokes.setup: Belly dancer appears at party
jokes.punchline: Leslie pretending this is a coincidence while clearly having hired entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of party chaos with Leslie's transparent lie.

jokes.text: You know, people hear about a killer party at Knope's house, they all come running.
jokes.setup: Multiple unexpected entertainers at party
jokes.punchline: Leslie claiming her reputation draws random performers
jokes.explanation: Leslie's delusional explanation for obvious hired entertainment.

jokes.text: Yeah. He's actually kind of awesome. You're underage.
jokes.setup: April agreeing Justin is awesome
jokes.punchline: Andy's blunt reminder about alcohol laws
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Andy's unexpected responsibility about underage drinking.

jokes.text: When it comes to choosing accounting software, there's no accounting for taste.
jokes.setup: Teacher demonstrating accounting class
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on 'accounting'
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad pun meant to show why accounting class should be cut - weak comedy by design.

jokes.text: But I can't, because Leslie loves using people for her own gain.
jokes.setup: Tom saying he'd defend Leslie
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with harsh truth
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment - his honesty overriding social politeness even in formal hearing.

jokes.text: I put a beautiful man in front of an adorable man-child. You're ruining it. I put a big white stallion in front of a little brown pony. Totally uncalled for.
jokes.setup: Leslie apologizing with metaphors
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly offensive animal comparisons
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Leslie's metaphors becoming increasingly problematic. Tom's reactions perfect.

jokes.text: Were you aware that all of the entertainment and food was provided by rec center teachers? Would I have stayed if I knew that? I don't know, would you have? Would you have?
jokes.setup: Formal hearing questioning Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron answering every question with a question, creating circular confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - his anti-government philosophy in action, creating absurd bureaucratic loop.

jokes.text: Oh. No. I'm sorry. We're closed, due to betrayment.
jokes.setup: Someone asking for shoe shine
jokes.punchline: Andy's made-up word 'betrayment' instead of 'betrayal'
jokes.explanation: Good Andy malapropism - his emotional state creating linguistic confusion.

jokes.text: Wow. Unbetrayed.
jokes.setup: Learning Justin didn't betray Leslie
jokes.punchline: Andy's attempt to reverse his made-up word
jokes.explanation: Good callback to previous malapropism - Andy trying to correct his linguistic error.

jokes.text: I had an awesome time which had nothing to do with the rec center teachers. No further questions.
jokes.setup: Justin testifying about the party
jokes.punchline: Leslie's legal-drama style conclusion to romantic testimony
jokes.explanation: Leslie treating romantic validation like legal victory - good character consistency.

jokes.text: It's every girl's dream to ask a dude how their date went under penalty of perjury.
jokes.setup: Ann asking why Leslie requested the hearing
jokes.punchline: Leslie's absurd romantic fantasy involving legal proceedings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie moment - perfectly captures her unique blend of romance and bureaucracy. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: there is now an official government document that proves my dinner party kicked ass.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her real motivation
jokes.punchline: Wanting bureaucratic validation for social success
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Leslie's character - needing official documentation for personal validation. Pure Leslie logic.

jokes.text: I'm not gay, but you're the most incredible man I've ever met. That doesn't sound gay at all.
jokes.setup: Tom expressing admiration for Justin
jokes.punchline: Justin pointing out the obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good comedic observation about Tom's unintentionally romantic declaration.
